title: Client API Key Exposure in Provider Calls
essence: API keys passed through client requests can be exposed in logs and error messages
symptoms: API keys visible in browser network requests
repro_steps: Inspect network request/response in browser dev tools
Check server logs for key exposure
root_cause: Client API keys are passed through request chain and may be logged or exposed in error responses without proper sanitization
do_not_do: Log raw API keys from client requests
Store client keys in browser localStorage
Pass keys through multiple service layers
guardrails: Sanitize API keys in all logging
Never include keys in error responses
Use server-side key management when possible
prevent_tests: Test error responses with client API keys

evidence.notes: Critical security issue - API keys should never be exposed to clients
